repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
shelfmark_ssi: UTO Sinai Greek NF M 90, SPP No. 362
contents_tesim: There are eight occurrences in the text of rubricated headings for the hours, indicating both the hour and the text to be read (e.g., Gospel, Acts) | There are no earlier attestations of this Horologion text
full_text_tesim: The folios from this object were originally independent bifolios sewn together from a much smaller Ethiopian book to form a bifolio for the new Greek book, oriented at 90 degrees to the overtext. The two bifolios were from an Ethiopian book whose size was approximately half the size of the new Greek book, thus when sewn together and trimmed, they provided a sheet of the right size and shape for the new Greek book. The seam is now in the gutter of the Greek book in its current shape. The gutter folds of the original Ethiopic sheets are still visible, oriented at 90 degrees to the orientation of the current Greek codex

There are eight occurrences in the text of rubricated headings for the hours, indicating both the hour and the text to be read (e.g., Gospel, Acts) | There are no earlier attestations of this Horologion text
UTO Sinai Greek NF M 90, SPP No. 362
Written in a medium-sized hand with average competence
